name: Northwest Michigan Community Action Agency, INC
description_short: Non-profit community action agency offering free housing repairs, weatherization, financial counseling, and homeless prevention across 10 Michigan counties.
description_long:
Northwest Michigan Community Action Agency (NMCAA) is a established non-profit organization that celebrated its 50th anniversary in April 2024. Operating across a 10-county region in Michigan with a staff of 300 employees, NMCAA positions itself as one of Michigan's leading community action organizations dedicated to supporting individuals through various life stages. The organization has invested over $113.5 million in community programs from 2019 to 2023, demonstrating significant operational scale and community commitment.

NMCAA offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including Head Start programming, Meals on Wheels, home repair, weatherization services, homeless prevention programs, and financial management counseling. Their Housing and Energy Efficiency Services program specifically helped 436 households and spent $6.19 million from 2023-2025. The organization operates an interactive county-based service locator and maintains a Homeownership and Financial Center alongside child and family development programs and food assistance initiatives.

The organization distinguishes itself through its regional multi-county coverage model, substantial annual budget deployment, and integration of multiple service lines addressing housing security, food access, energy efficiency, and financial capability. NMCAA actively maintains modern digital presence across multiple social platforms and provides accessibility features on their website including screen-reader compatibility. Their 50-year operational history suggests stability and established community partnerships.

As a legitimate non-profit, NMCAA aligns with the free-help category and serves populations seeking assistance rather than credit products. Potential users should note that while services are described as free or low-cost, eligibility requirements likely apply based on income and geographic location within their 10-county service area. The website lacks detailed pricing transparency or specific eligibility criteria documentation.
pros: Established 50-year non-profit with $113.5M invested in community programs (2019-2023)
Serves 10-county region with 300 employees, providing broad geographic reach in Michigan
Free or low-cost services including emergency home repair, weatherization, and financial counseling
Multi-program approach addressing housing, food security, energy efficiency, and financial management simultaneously
Helped 436 households with $6.19M in housing/energy services (2023-2025)
Operating Homeownership and Financial Center for financial capability building
Accessible website with screen-reader compatibility and modern digital communication
cons: Limited geographic coverage restricted to 10 specific Michigan counties only
Website lacks clear eligibility requirements and income thresholds for services
No pricing transparency or detailed service application process documented online
No mention of financial counseling certifications (HUD-approval or NFCC status not stated)
Contact form required to inquire about programs rather than direct service descriptions
best_for: Low-income Michigan residents in the 10-county service area seeking free home repair or weatherization
Families experiencing homelessness or housing instability needing prevention services
Individuals seeking free financial management and homeownership counseling
Households wanting emergency home repairs or energy efficiency improvements
diagnosis: NMCAA is best for low-income Michigan residents in its 10-county service area (Antrim, Benzie, Charlevoix, Emmet, Grand Traverse, Kalkaska, Leelanau, Missaukee, Roscommon, Wexford) seeking free community action assistance with housing, energy, food, or financial counseling. The primary caveat is that services are geographically limited and likely have income-based eligibility requirements not detailed on the website; prospective users must contact the agency directly to determine qualification.
services: Meals on Wheels food delivery for seniors
Homeownership counseling and financial center services
County-based program locator and eligibility assessment
Community impact and resource navigation services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3963 3 Mile Rd N, Traverse City, MI 49686
